events.label: reasoning
events.text:
Brazing without nitrogen purge creates black scale (copper oxide) inside the lines. This is a serious defect - the scale will flake off and damage the compressor, clog capillary tubes/TXV, and contaminate the system. This is a sequence violation because nitrogen purge must happen during brazing, not after. The work is in-progress and the brazing has already been done incorrectly.

The system needs to be reworked - the brazed joints need to be cut out and re-brazed with nitrogen purge, or the lines need to be cleaned/replaced. This is a fail because it's non-compliant with manufacturer specs and industry standards.

Let me construct the JSON response.

    "Perform system flush with appropriate solvent if partial contamination is suspected, then verify cleanliness with inspection",
    "Complete proper evacuation to below 500 microns with a micron gauge before charging",
    "Leak test all brazed joints before final charge"
  ],
  "rationale": "Brazing copper without an inert nitrogen purge creates internal copper oxide scale that cannot be removed by evacuation. This is a manufacturer and industry standard requirement — the sequence is wrong because nitrogen must be flowing during the braze. The installed line set is defective and non-compliant; it must be reworked before evacuation can proceed.",
